topic: A neighbor wants to install a fence
raw_output:
[story 1]

The hammering started precisely at seven this morning, a relentless, jarring rhythm that sliced through my morning quiet. I was trying to enjoy my coffee, the steam warming my face, when the noise began. I glanced out the window, and there he was, standing on his lawn, wielding a power saw with unnecessary vigor. Every thud of the tool against the wood sent a spike of tension through my shoulders. I set my mug down a little too hard on the counter, the clink echoing the sudden sharpness in my chest. I could feel my jaw clenching, the muscles in my neck tightening as I watched him work, seemingly oblivious to the disturbance he was causing in my peace.

[story 2]

"Are you quite finished with that racket?" I called out, my voice pitched a little higher than I intended. I stood by the mailbox, arms crossed tightly over my chest, trying to project a calm I absolutely did not feel. The man next door paused, wiping sweat from his brow with the back of a gloved hand, and offered a tight, somewhat strained smile. "Just getting this done, neighbor. It'll be up and running soon." His tone was overly cheerful, which only made the rapid-fire sawing sound seem more grating. I shifted my weight from one foot to the other, my gaze fixed on the uneven line of the new fence posts, a sour taste rising in my throat.

[story 3]

I spent the entire afternoon pacing the length of my driveway. Each time the sound of the hammer resumed, I stopped dead in my tracks, my hands instinctively gripping the railing of the porch. It was a constant, low-level thrumming that refused to dissipate. I kept rubbing my temples, trying to physically smooth out the fraying edges of my concentration. When he finally stopped for a break, stretching his back with an audible groan, I just stared at him, my expression carefully blank, though the tight set of my lips betrayed the sheer weariness of enduring the spectacle.
